reason: SSE replay via /sync/* not implemented in Phase A
/experimental/* endpoints best-effort; not gated until Phase D

Provider OAuth is implemented for built-in providers (plan 13, #109): PKCE auth-code flow over a shared 127.0.0.1 loopback callback with CSRF state, token exchange → shared auth.json (xAI is the first provider; --oauth-callback-proxy-url for headless). Not yet byte-parity-gated by a dual-run scenario (the flow opens a browser + needs live provider creds). Deferred: token refresh and additional providers.
track: done for built-in providers (plan 13); refresh + more providers later

reason: GET /provider is implemented (models.dev catalog + connected/default). It is NOT byte-parity with opencode: `all` matches, but `connected` depends on which provider credentials are in the daemon's env/auth.json (opencode also detects via per-provider SDK init). Membership/ids are comparable; not gated by a dual-run scenario.
track: done (plan 04 M7/M8); refine oauth detection later

reason: GET /agent is implemented (built-ins + .opencode/agent(s) + config). It is NOT byte-parity with opencode: built-in agents carry a simple allow-all permission ruleset rather than opencode's env-specific patterns (tool-output/temp dirs) and full system prompts, Opcode42 omits flag-gated built-ins (scout), and the config `agent` overlay applies only description/mode/hidden/model (not prompt/temperature/options/permission). The self-diff gate is opencode-vs-opencode so this does not affect CI.
track: done (plan 04 M3/M8); richer built-in permissions later

reason: GET /command is implemented (.opencode/command(s) + config). ORDERING is no longer a divergence: opencode returns the list in non-deterministic (map/glob) order, Opcode42 sorts by name (a recorded known-addition, masterplan decision #6), and the `command-list` conformance scenario compares the body order-insensitively (set-normalized, client.go orderInsensitiveListPath), so the self-diff gate covers /command. The REMAINING divergence is content, and applies only to a opcode42-vs-opencode dual-run: Opcode42 returns only file/config commands (source=command) while opencode also surfaces built-in, MCP, and skill-derived commands — so opencode's command SET is a superset of Opcode42's.
track: ordering done (decision #6, order-insensitive scenario); skill/MCP/built-in commands are plan 04 M6 / plan 03

reason: GET /find/file approximates opencode two ways, so it is not gated by a parity scenario: (1) ignore semantics — opencode delegates to `rg --files` (full .gitignore); Opcode42 uses a fixed ignore set (.git/node_modules/vendor/.opcode42 + hidden), so results may include files .gitignore would exclude. (2) ranking — opencode uses fuzzysort; Opcode42 uses its own subsequence scorer, so result ORDER differs (membership is comparable, order is not). The missing-query 400 body is Opcode42's {_tag,message} rather than opencode's Effect Query error.
track: TODO: optional — vendor a .gitignore-aware walker for exact membership parity (plan 04)

reason: The `task` subagent tool is wired (child session linked to parent, nested agent loop, last-text result wrapped in opencode's <task>/<task_result> envelope). One parity gap: opencode also disables `todowrite` on the child unless the agent's permission grants it (tool/task.ts:194); Opcode42 only disables `task` (nil subagent → bounded recursion). Honoring per-message tool disables needs PromptInput.Tools wired into the registry filter (not yet consumed), so it is deferred. Child session titles also use the generic timestamp rather than opencode's '<desc> (@agent subagent)'.
track: TODO: wire per-message Tools gating + descriptive child titles (plan 02 follow-up)

reason: System instructions from AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/CONTEXT.md (project upward-walk + global ~/.config/opencode + ~/.claude) and local config `instructions[]` are resolved into the system prompt. http(s) URLs in `instructions[]` are NOT fetched (opencode fetches them; instruction.ts:154-159) — remote instruction fetch is deferred. The `disable_claude_code_prompt` flag is also not honored yet (CLAUDE.md is always a candidate).
track: TODO: fetch remote instruction URLs + honor disable_claude_code_prompt (plan 04)

reason: GET /skill loads skills from: .opencode {skill,skills}/**/SKILL.md (project + ~/.config/opencode), the external ~/.claude and ~/.agents skills/**/SKILL.md dirs (global + project found-up), and config skills.paths[] (**/SKILL.md). Name from frontmatter (no-name skipped), content = body, last-wins dedup. Remaining gaps vs opencode: (1) no built-in/embedded skills (opencode bundles e.g. customize-opencode, location '<built-in>'); (2) no remote skills.urls[] discovery/pull + cache; (3) the disableExternalSkills/disableClaudeCodeSkills flags aren't honored. Not gated by a parity scenario.
track: TODO: embed built-in skills + remote skills.urls[] pull (plan 04 M6)

reason: Credential CRUD is implemented: PUT/DELETE /auth/{providerID} read+write opencode's shared ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json (api/oauth/wellknown records), so a key added in Opcode42 flips /provider.connected and is shared with opencode. The OAuth authorize/callback flow is now implemented for built-in providers (plan 13, #109: PKCE auth-code over a shared loopback callback, token exchange → auth.json). Deferred: GET /provider/auth (the per-provider auth-method listing), token refresh, and additional OAuth providers.
track: OAuth done for built-in providers (plan 13); GET /provider/auth method listing + refresh later (plan 04 M7/M8)

reason: The websearch tool is wired against Exa/Parallel hosted MCP endpoints (env-gated: OPENCODE_WEBSEARCH_PROVIDER / EXA_API_KEY / PARALLEL_API_KEY); without a key it returns a clear 'not configured' error. Opcode42 passes only the query (defaults: type=auto, numResults=8, livecrawl=fallback); it does not forward opencode's numResults/livecrawl/type/contextMaxCharacters params, the session_id/model_name to Parallel, or apply opencode's session-checksum default-provider selection (Opcode42 defaults to whichever key is present). It is not behind a permission ask.
track: TODO: forward full search params + session/model context (plan 04)

reason: MCP (plan 03): config (mcp.* local/remote, incl. the oauth false|object|absent discriminator) is parsed per-instance; BOTH local (stdio) and remote servers connect lazily — remote uses StreamableHTTP with SSE fallback (opencode's transport order, mcp/index.ts:339-413); GET /mcp reports the full {connected|disabled|failed|needs_auth|needs_client_registration} status union (a remote 401 with OAuth not disabled drives DCR and surfaces needs_auth, or needs_client_registration when the server has no registration endpoint and no configured clientId — mcp/index.ts:360-414); tools are merged into the agent loop — flattened as sanitize(server)_sanitize(tool), exposed to the model, and dispatched via the executor (registry-miss → MCP); a notifications/tools/list_changed watcher re-lists tools and emits the mcp.tools.changed bus event; and MCP tool execution is permission-gated through the same ask/Ask path as built-ins (keyed on the flattened tool name, patterns/always '*', emitting permission.asked — asserted by conformance/mcp_permission_test.go). The mutating endpoints are now implemented (no longer 501): POST /mcp (add), POST /mcp/:name/connect, POST /mcp/:name/disconnect, POST /mcp/:name/auth (start → {authorizationUrl, oauthState}), POST /mcp/:name/auth/callback (finish with pasted code → Status), POST /mcp/:name/auth/authenticate, DELETE /mcp/:name/auth, with opencode's McpServerNotFoundError (404) / McpUnsupportedOAuthError (400) error shapes. OAuth reuses RFC 8414 discovery + RFC 7591 DCR + PKCE via mark3labs/mcp-go's OAuth handler; tokens persist to opencode's shared $DATA/opencode/mcp-auth.json (same file/shape opencode uses, so credentials are interoperable). Remaining divergence: opencode's authAuthenticate opens a browser on the daemon host and blocks for its loopback callback; Opcode42's daemon is headless, so authAuthenticate starts the flow and returns needs_auth — the caller opens the authorization URL (from POST /mcp/:name/auth) and posts the code back to /auth/callback. Token refresh on expiry is mcp-go's responsibility (refresh_token grant) and is not yet exercised by a dual-run scenario (the flow needs a live OAuth server).
track: done (plan 03 M3-2): OAuth statuses + mutating endpoints; headless authAuthenticate + dual-run OAuth scenario later

reason: PATCH /session/{id} (session.update) is implemented at full parity for the normal contract: partial title/time.archived updates, full-Session 200 response, 404 NotFoundError on a missing id, 400 BadRequest on an empty/absent body or a wrong-typed/non-object payload, unknown top-level fields ignored (not rejected), and time.archived only ever SET by a number (null/absent are no-ops; opencode has no un-archive path). The session-update dual-run scenario (rename -> archive -> archived:null no-op) diffs clean. ONE deliberate divergence, not exercised by any scenario: for a SYNTACTICALLY MALFORMED JSON body (e.g. '{"title":') opencode returns 500 UnknownError (an uncaught decode throw specific to this endpoint), whereas Opcode42 returns the more correct 400 BadRequest. Emitting a 500 for a client error would be harmful, so Opcode42 does not mirror it.

Plan 17 Workstream C: the TUI in-chat inline diff (rendered at edit/apply_patch tool completion) uses the theme's DiffPalette background tints (AddedBg/RemovedBg/ContextBg) for added/removed/context rows. opencode's run mini-TUI uses transparent diff backgrounds (run/theme.ts:572-574); opencode's full-TUI diff-viewer plugin uses real tints. Opcode42 chose the tints for the inline diff for readability and colorblind-safety (the +/- sign marker column carries the color cue the background tint alone does not). This is a visual divergence from the run mini-TUI only; the wire shape (metadata.diff for edit, metadata.files[].patch for apply_patch) and the snapshot title ('# Edited <file>' / '# Created|Deleted|Moved|Patched <file>') match opencode (scrollback.writer.tsx:188-225, tool.ts:484-498, 515-569). Not gated by a parity scenario (TUI-only, no daemon wire).
track: intentional: tints (Opcode42) vs transparent (opencode run mini-TUI) for the inline diff bg

reason: Plan 17 Workstream C: Opcode42's TUI keeps the per-tool fold toggle (ctrl+x v) for the inline diff — collapsed shows only the header ('Edit <file> ▸'), expanded shows header + diff. opencode's run mini-TUI has no per-tool fold for the inline diff (the snapshot always renders in full). This is an intentional extension to the run mini-TUI behavior, mirroring opencode's full-TUI diff-viewer plugin (which does have per-file fold). Documented in plans/17-tui-scroll-layout.md §C5.
track: intentional: per-tool fold toggle (Opcode42 extension, ctrl+x v) for the inline diff

reason: TUI (plan 17 §D1): Opcode42 binds ctrl+x r (toggle hide/show ThinkingMode) and ctrl+x f (toggle the per-reasoning expanded signal). opencode's full TUI leaves `display_thinking` unbound (`keybind("none")`, packages/tui/src/config/keybind.ts:150), reachable only via the `/thinking` command palette. Opcode42 binding it to a chord is a convenience divergence — opencode users had no keybind at all and had to use the slash command. No wire change; reasoning parts always render at least a 1-line header (matching opencode's full TUI showThinking = createMemo(() => true), packages/tui/src/routes/session/index.tsx:254), so this only affects which affordance the user has to toggle the visibility, not what's rendered.
track: intentional: convenience keybind for a palette-only action in opencode

reason: TUI (plan 17 §D1): Opcode42 defaults `hideThinking = true` (collapsed, 1-line header). This MATCHES opencode's full-TUI default ("hide", packages/tui/src/context/thinking.ts:36) — not a divergence from opencode's full TUI. Logged here as a divergence from opencode's RUN mini-TUI default ("show"), which is what the original plan 17 wording picked up before §D1 corrected it: the run mini-TUI (packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run/session-data.ts:906-908) DROPS the reasoning part entirely when !input.thinking, while Opcode42's full TUI collapses it to a header. Opcode42 follows opencode's full TUI behavior, NOT the run mini-TUI behavior.
track: intentional: matches opencode full-TUI default ("hide"); diverges from run mini-TUI default ("show" + drop)
